content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the right ahead with the car positioned slightly left of center on the curve entry, and the right-hand bend is clearly visible with the dashed center line curving away.

Target waypoint: nearest_waypoints[1] + 5 = 18 + 5 = 23
Waypoint 23: (6.502514, 0.718808)
Car position: (5.733551, 0.654101)
Bearing = atan2(0.718808 - 0.654101, 6.502514 - 5.733551) = atan2(0.064707, 0.768963) ≈ 4.81°
Current heading: -1.165°
Delta = 4.81 - (-1.165) = 5.975° → slight left needed

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves right ahead as visible in image; delta to waypoint 23 is +5.97° (slight left), so apply minimal 2.5° left steering to track the waypoint line into the right curve without oscillating.",

    "Track starts with a long straight section heading in +X direction along y≈0.683",
    "Large right-hand curve begins around waypoint 24-30 (y increases significantly)",
    "Car is nearly centered on the straight, delta near 5-6° as curve approaches",
    "Use steering_angle=2.5 for delta 5-10° to avoid oscillation",
    "After the right curve, track loops back left and returns — full circuit",
    "Speed 1.5 is max and appropriate for straight sections and gentle curves",
    "Waypoints 0-23 are nearly perfectly straight along y≈0.683",
    "Right curve starts gently at wp24 (y increases), sharpens at wp27-30",
    "Car heading stays near -1.1° on the straight — minimal correction needed",
    "Delta ~6° at wp23 suggests beginning to track into the right curve soon"
  ],

  },
  "waypoint_analysis": 5.97,
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": 2.5
}
```
